figures.caption: Figure 2. Qualitative comparison of SST downscaling methods for a representative test day (31 January 2011, randomly selected for visualization). Top row (a-d): reconstructed SST fields from U-Net, RandOrthDiff-K40, PODiff-K40, and ROMS ground truth. Bottom row (e-g): corresponding signed reconstruction errors (prediction minus truth). PODiff-K40 achieves the lowest reconstruction errors, particularly in regions of strong thermal gradients and close to the coast. The RandOrthDiff employs the same diffusion architecture and latent dimensionality as PODiff but replaces POD modes with a random orthonormal basis for a controlled comparison of latent representations.

Figure 7. Spatial maps of empirical coverage minus nominal coverage for PODiff-K40 at the 50% and 90% confidence levels, averaged over the test set. Warm (cool) colors indicate overcoverage (undercoverage).
